hero.eyebrow: Fish Restaurant · Danube Bank, Novi Sad
hero.title: River fish, done the old way
hero.subtitle: Carp, catfish, and pike-perch from local fishermen, a fish soup recipe that hasn't changed since 1987, and a terrace twenty steps from the water.
hero.imageAlt: Terrace tables overlooking the Danube at sunset

services.title: From our kitchen
services.subtitle: A short menu that follows the river and the season.

items.title: Fish soup
items.description: The house classic, simmered for hours in a kettle over open fire. Served with homemade bread and a warning: it's spicy.

items.title: Grilled river fish
items.description: Whole carp, catfish steaks, and pike-perch fillets over charcoal, with garlic, parsley, and nothing that hides the fish.

items.title: Seasonal sides & salads
items.description: Whatever the market had that morning — young potatoes, chard, tomato salad in summer, pickled peppers in winter.

items.title: Local wines & rakija
items.description: A short list from Fruška Gora wineries, plus the quince rakija our neighbour makes and we refuse to reveal from whom.

about.title: The house
about.subtitle: Three generations by the same stretch of river.
about.body: Đorđe Šaranović opened the čarda in 1987 with four tables and one kettle. His son runs the kitchen today; his granddaughter runs the floor. The menu is short on purpose — we'd rather do eight dishes properly than forty from a freezer. Fish comes from local fishermen we've known for decades, and when the river gives nothing, we say so instead of serving yesterday's catch.

items.title: Terrace by the water
items.description: Covered and heated in the shoulder seasons.

items.title: Reservations by phone
items.description: Weekends fill up by Thursday — call ahead.

items.title: Kids welcome
items.description: High chairs, a smaller fish plate, and space to run.

items.title: Parking on site
items.description: Free parking and a bike rack by the entrance.

items.alt: Fish soup served in a small kettle
items.caption: The famous kettle

items.alt: Grilled carp on a wooden board
items.caption: Carp from the charcoal

items.alt: Glasses of white wine on a checkered tablecloth
items.caption: Fruška Gora whites

items.name: Vladimir K.
items.text: I've eaten fish soup on three continents. The one here is the standard I judge all the others by.

items.name: Elena & Paolo
items.text: Our hotel receptionist whispered this address like a secret. Now we understand why. Order the catfish.

items.name: Nikola P.
items.text: Forty people, tamburica until midnight, and not a single thing to complain about. They treat you like family.

pricing.title: From the menu
pricing.subtitle: The short version — the full menu waits at the table. Prices per portion.

plans.name: Pike-perch fillet
plans.description: The gentle one, kids love it

items.question: Do I need a reservation?
items.answer: Weekdays you'll usually find a table. Friday and Saturday evenings, and any sunny weekend afternoon, book by phone a few days ahead — the terrace goes first.

items.question: Is there anything for people who don't eat fish?
items.answer: A few grilled meat dishes and honest vegetable sides. But we'll be straight with you: people come here for the fish.

items.question: How spicy is the fish soup, really?
items.answer: Properly spicy — it's an alas soup, that's the point. Ask for the mild kettle if you're unsure; we make both without judgment.

items.question: Can you host a celebration?
items.answer: Up to sixty people with the terrace, forty inside. For groups over fifteen we agree on a set menu in advance so the kitchen can do it right.

cta.title: The terrace is waiting
cta.subtitle: Call to reserve — especially if you want the tables by the railing.

contact.title: Find us on the bank
contact.subtitle: On the Danube bank, a ten-minute drive from the centre.

footer.description: Family fish restaurant on the Danube since 1987.
